body:

# ![][1]

Our American values are on the line -- and our country’s future depends on how
we fight back. It’s going to take millions of Americans coming together to
raise their voices, together. That’s why Kamala is running for president: to
fight for truth, decency, justice, and equality.

Thanks for signing up to join Kamala’s team today. Here are some things you
can do next to support our campaign:

* Follow Kamala on [**Facebook**][2], [**Twitter**][3] and [**Instagram**][4] for real-time updates.
* Forward this email to five of your friends and family and encourage them to get involved.
* Represent Team Kamala with some [**awesome campaign merch.**][5]
* [**Give a donation of $10 or more today.**][6] Our campaign doesn’t accept a dime from corporate PACs, so we’re relying on grassroots contributions from people like you to power our movement. Every donation -- no matter what amount -- truly makes a difference.

Thanks again for joining Kamala’s campaign for president. We’re so happy to
have you on our team.

Talk soon,

Team Kamala

![][7]

---

PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE

because you are subscribed to Senator Kamala Harris' mailing list.

subject: I am constantly humbled

#
One of the ways you can see our momentum in this presidential primary is watching the crowd at our events. Just the other day in Iowa, nearly 1,000 people joined me for a town hall in the middle of a flood warning. It was another remarkable night for our campaign where we talked about my plans to raise teacher salaries, cut taxes for working families, and finally address our country's gun violence epidemic. **I am constantly humbled by supporters like you who not only show up to events, but volunteer and contribute in so many ways to this movement of, by, and for the people. I know we are going to win this primary and defeat Donald Trump because of YOU.** But first, we need an influx of donations to ensure our team has the resources to build a winning campaign in all 50 states. name: Andrew Yang
subject: Welcome to Yang2020
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend -- Thank you for joining our fight for humanity. Bringing big solutions to fix the massive problems facing our country will not happen without people like you. Over the next couple weeks, I will email you two more times to share why I am running for president, introduce the Freedom Dividend, and provide a _real_ plan for how our campaign can continue to shock the world. Additionally, I will make sure you receive my weekly campaign updates, which I write personally from the road. In the meantime, here are some key resources for the campaign you might enjoy:
* How to reach out to me:
* If you want to share something, find me on [Twitter](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=YqgukYA3Mrlri-dqG7I8TqE6xdNTSVfW5RhtXxhMjJ0&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=041219_welcome&n=1) or [Instagram](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=6hyvAYXBs1PELL28GD73y48vYg4fs9ZaLi2ukqvLwvI9bRvyHXcaIea7s5S94IF6&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=041219_welcome&n=2). name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: They tried to silence our message but failed
body:
| | Peter, You know how every time a conservative commentator or news site (or anti-abortion movie such as _Unplanned_) gets blocked from social media or search results, it’s always the fault of some totally “nonpartisan algorithm”? Even the big tech billionaires swore under oath to Congress that their companies do not manipulate the results to silence people’s free speech. Perish the thought! Well, at least someone’s looking into it. This past Wednesday the Senate Judiciary Committee held a hearing regarding alleged censorship of conservatives on Twitter and Facebook platforms. **This comes after Twitter suspended the _Unplanned_ movie account for an hour after the movie hit theaters.** And Twitter’s Director of Public Policy did admit to downgrading posts and purposely limiting viewers in some situations. He wouldn’t call it “shadow banning,” but let’s be honest, that’s exactly what it is. Prior to this morning and only because mainstream media finally started to report on this, Google had the gall to categorize the real life of Abby Johnson as "Propaganda". [](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190412apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190412apl01nd&n=1) But despite Twitter’s censorship of _Unplanned_ and Google's mischaracterization, YOU have helped ensure the movie was a huge success. It brought in over $12 million at the box office so far and most importantly, it’s changing people’s hearts and minds on abortion. **Thanks to My Faith Votes supporters, the technology we provided at the end of the movie to help those impacted by abortion and to activate people to take action in the pro-life cause has already assisted more than 7,700 people!** We’re advancing people’s view on the sanctity of life, but it’s evident we must work against the mainstream culture of Hollywood and the discrimination from social media platforms. For example, in Georgia, the heartbeat bill, banning abortion after the baby has a detectable heartbeat, will be signed into law despite the petitions from over 100 liberal actors and comedians threatening to boycott the state. And Ohio followed Georgia’s lead and also passed into law the Heartbeat Bill. **But we can’t stop or slow down. While some states are working to protect the unborn, states like New York and Virginia are actually celebrating laws that not only allow late term abortion but permit infanticide.** That’s why My Faith Votes is leading a national movement equipping and mobilizing Christians to stand for life in the 85,000 elections taking place in 2019 and the 100,000 elections in 2020. Ask yourself this, which candidates seeking to move into the White House are pro-life? I can count them on zero fingers. On this crucial issue, the choice Americans have before them in 2020 will be stark, and we can’t leave it up to social media platforms and Hollywood to hold the megaphone. We must mobilize Christians. This is why we need your financial support to reach the roughly 25 million Christians who don’t show up to vote in presidential elections. Make no mistake about it, we have an enormous opportunity to bring the influence of our faith to every election and transform our nation. [I’m asking everyone this month to consider making a contribution to reach Christians to stand for life at the polls](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190412apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190412apl01nd&n=2). For about the price of a coffee you can activate a Christian voter. Equip one Christian to vote for $3, reach 5 Christians for a contribution of $15, and 20 for a donation of $60. I hope you will join us in this battle to see the sanctity of life protected. [](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190412apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190412apl01nd&n=3) **United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190412apl01nd&n=4)

Thanks to My Faith Votes supporters, the technology we provided at the end of the movie to help those impacted by abortion and to activate people to take action in the pro-life cause has already assisted more than 7,700 people! We’re advancing people’s view on the sanctity of life, but it’s evident we must work against the mainstream culture of Hollywood and the discrimination from social media platforms. For example, in Georgia, the heartbeat bill, banning abortion after the baby has a detectable heartbeat, will be signed into law despite the petitions from over 100 liberal actors and comedians threatening to boycott the state. And Ohio followed Georgia’s lead and also passed into law the Heartbeat Bill. But we can’t stop or slow down. While some states are working to protect the unborn, states like New York and Virginia are actually celebrating laws that not only allow late term abortion but permit infanticide. That’s why My Faith Votes is leading a national movement equipping and mobilizing Christians to stand for life in the 85,000 elections taking place in 2019 and the 100,000 elections in 2020. Ask yourself this, which candidates seeking to move into the White House are pro-life? I can count them on zero fingers. On this crucial issue, the choice Americans have before them in 2020 will be stark, and we can’t leave it up to social media platforms and Hollywood to hold the megaphone. We must mobilize Christians. This is why we need your financial support to reach the roughly 25 million Christians who don’t show up to vote in presidential elections. Make no mistake about it, we have an enormous opportunity to bring the influence of our faith to every election and transform our nation. . For about the price of a coffee you can activate a Christian voter. Equip one Christian to vote for $3, reach 5 Christians for a contribution of $15, and 20 for a donation of $60. I hope you will join us in this battle to see the sanctity of life protected. United. We Stand. Gov. Mike Huckabee Honorary National Chairman * ©2019 My Faith Votes 10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

subject: Can I explain?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:

#
Okay -- here’s the truth: I am going to ask you to make a donation to my campaign in just a few moments. It’s not my favorite thing about running for president, but it’s important and I hope you will let me explain why it matters so much. I’ll start here: I am not taking a dime from corporate PACs or federal lobbyists -- and that was a very deliberate choice. **It means we are intentionally leaving resources on the table.** But that’s ok with me. I never want there to be any question about whether I’m listening to the people or special interests. That’s why the average donation to fundraising messages like this one is generally about $20 -- and why I believe grassroots donors are going to sustain our movement into this primary. Here’s the other reason: next week, I am huddling with my team to evaluate the budget and our plans to build a winning campaign. The more money we raise this week, the more we can compete -- especially in early states like Iowa, South Carolina, Nevada, and New Hampshire. The choices we make in this meeting will set the tone and pace for the rest of our campaign. I am running to win -- so we are pushing our team as hard as we can because there is just no other option. **Can I count on you to add a contribution to help reach our fundraising goal of $250,000 online this week? name: ElizabethWarren.com
subject: Thanks for your input
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
| | |
---
Thanks for being a part of the conversation about how we can make big, structural change in our country.

For Elizabeth, the fight to level the playing field for working families is personal. When she was growing up in Oklahoma, her family barely hung on by their fingernails. Her dad had a heart attack when she was 12, and her mom saved their home by getting a minimum wage job answering the phones at Sears. Elizabeth graduated from a public commuter college that cost $50 a semester, and it opened a million doors for her.

She went on to become a public school teacher, law professor, United States Senator, and now a presidential candidate because she grew up at a time when America invested in kids like her.

But here's the hard truth: America isn't building that kind of future any longer. Today, the game is rigged - rigged to work for those who have money and power. And for some, the game has always been rigged against them.

This dark path doesn't have to be our future. **We can make our democracy work for all of us. We can make our economy work for all of us. We can rebuild America's middle class - but this time, we have to build it for everyone.**

Elizabeth is grateful for America's promise to invest in kids like her - and she always says she's determined down to her toes to keep that promise for every kid in this country.

As a law professor studying bankruptcy, she spent decades uncovering the financial industry's tricks and traps. She's spent her career standing up to giant Wall Street banks and powerful interests, and working to give everyone a fighting chance at success. And she didn't stop when she got to the United States Senate. Now, she's keeping up the fight by running for President.

But this isn't just her fight. It's going to take every single one of us to save our democracy, rebuild the middle class, and end Washington corruption - and your voice matters.

By working together - by listening to and learning from each other - we'll make sure we're ready to fight and win together.

**[If that's a fight you're interested in being a part of, and if you're ready to say you're all in to support Elizabeth, add your name here.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f38d/45c30650/b3b9de7c/66948ce5/1087113347/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiJ9/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ws-ns-allin?source=ws-nd-ns-non-em1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=")**

Thank you for being in this fight,

Team Warren

subject: From ‘No Crisis’ to ‘Breaking Point’: Mainstream Media Changes its Tune
body:
"Months after repeatedly dismissing and mocking President Trump’s claim of a national emergency at the Southern U.S. border, the mainstream media..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ac0c7728aa&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **From ‘No Crisis’ to ‘Breaking Point’: Mainstream Media Changes its Tune**

“Months after repeatedly dismissing and mocking President Trump’s claim of a national emergency at the Southern U.S. border, the mainstream media are grappling with reality,” Lukas Mikelionis writes for Fox News.

But it’s worth remembering that as recently as February, a _New York Times_ “fact check” of President Donald J. Trump’s State of the Union address claimed that “President Trump described illegal border crossings as a ‘urgent national crisis.’ This is false.” Today, Mikelionis describes how “the same media outlets are now scrambling to accurately portray the border crisis, no longer fearing to quote top officials and their data, and declare that the southern border is at a ‘breaking point’ amid a surge in the number of illegal immigrant crossings.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6c57ca6727&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Tax Day is always bad because Americans are overtaxed. But this Tax Day, we can celebrate the fact that an overwhelming majority of people are being taxed less, thanks to the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, which Republicans passed in late 2017,” the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8d615569f9&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “There was a deliberate attempt to trick the public into believing the bill was a middle-class tax increase . . . [but] no fewer than four out of every five taxpayers saw a reduction thanks to the law.”
---
|
---
| “The Obama administration's Department of Homeland Security Secretary Jeh Johnson said Thursday on _Fox & Friends_ that the influx of immigrant caravans at the Mexico border was ‘a crisis by any measure,’” Jeffrey Cimmino reports in _[The Washington Free Beacon](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e027dbc7e2&e=3ee4985eea)_. “It requires a change in law,” Johnson said, calling for a bipartisan solution. A number of prominent Democrats continue to downplay the crisis, Cimmino writes.
---
|
---
| U.S. weekly jobless claims dropped to a near 50-year low last week, pointing to continued market strength, _[Reuters](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8f17463f6d&e=3ee4985eea)_ reports. “Initial claims for state unemployment benefits fell 8,000 to a seasonally adjusted 196,000 for the week ended April 6, the lowest level since early October 1969. Claims have now declined for four straight weeks.”
---
|

name: Team Spanberger
subject: She’s doing what she said she’d do -- She is working to lower prescription drug prices.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
Abigail is advocating for us in Congress, making sure that life-saving medication is affordable and accessible to all Americans, not just those who can afford it.
| | Peter --

The skyrocketing cost of prescription drugs has had a devastating impact on individuals and families across the country. There are people choosing between putting food on the table and buying their medication, attending to their chronic illnesses or paying rent, and making heartbreaking decisions because of the cost of medications.

We can, and we must, do better. **That 's why Abigail is proud to have introduced a bill that increases transparency and accountability for the pricing practices of Pharmacy Benefit Managers (or "PBMs").** The bill has already passed out of committee, with bipartisan support, which is the first step in getting it to the floor.

So what's a PBM? A PBM is a third-party group that negotiates prices between drug manufacturers, health insurers, and pharmacies. Most importantly, **their negotiations end up driving YOUR out-of-pocket costs**. So far, the information from these negotiations on rebates, discounts, and price concession has not been available for the public to see.

That's where Abigail's bill comes in. Her bipartisan proposal will bring transparency to a system where it is sorely needed. There's little doubt that the way PBM negotiations are currently run plays a central role in the skyrocketing costs of prescription drugs, but we cannot even quantify it because of the lack of transparency. This bill is a first critical step in addressing that oversight. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/B_BCw-xVoyQDD8QkXAi9d-q9scDY6uv4ehEiqihTno0=/4_E7E8bTjcSL8SHyvQMdI-7MNN2eP2rfrsmyLJoljts=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.12_b1_ts_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/B_BCw-xVoyQDD8QkXAi9d-q9scDY6uv4ehEiqihTno0=/4_E7E8bTjcSL8SHyvQMdI-7MNN2eP2rfrsmyLJoljts=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.12_b1_ts_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)
---
Abigail is advocating for us in Congress, making sure that life-saving medication is affordable and accessible to all Americans, not just those who can afford it. That's why in addition to leading the way with this legislation, **she also helped introduce a bill that would allow Medicare to negotiate its prescription drugs rates!** We're so proud of the progress Abigail's making in Congress and her willingness to put aside partisan games to deliver real results for the people of the 7th District.

**She 's been so effective, in fact, that Abigail's seat is now a top target. We need more dedicated public servants in Washington like Abigail who are working on behalf of the people, not special interests, and delivering on the promises they made.**

[**Peter, please, contribute to Abigail 's competitive re-election campaign tonight, and stand with her as she fights to end the skyrocketing costs of prescription drugs >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/B_BCw-xVoyQDD8QkXAi9d-q9scDY6uv4ehEiqihTno0=/4_E7E8bTjcSL8SHyvQMdI-7MNN2eP2rfrsmyLJoljts=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.12_b1_ts_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)

Together, I know we can continue to make life-saving progress.

Jake LaTurner

Thanks to your solid show of support, we can confidently report that the LaTurner for Senate campaign is powered by Kansas donors and not Washington D.C. PACs. Kansas conservatives are truly ready for a new generation of leadership that is willing to stand up and fight for them in Washington DC. Jake has the will to defend conservative principles and is not afraid to defend those principles alongside President Trump. Help us spread the word to your friends and neighbors by sharing Jake’s . name: John Hickenlooper
subject: The work of repair
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
It's important to reflect on where we've been | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1cb/522321320/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend,

When you're running for president, it's important to take time to reflect not just on where we're going as a country, but where we've been.

Taking an honest look at our past is the only way to ensure that we don't repeat it, and acknowledging wrongs is the first step toward healing them.

**That 's why last week, at the National Action Network conference, I called for a formal presidential apology for American slavery--for the shame, the sin, the injustice, and the permanent stain it has left on our history, and for the structural inequality and cruelty that endure as its legacy.**

In Colorado, our work of repair began by addressing police misconduct and bias, prioritizing criminal justice reform, tackling housing and employment discrimination, expanding access to the ballot, and emphasizing rehabilitation and treatment for drug use, rather than jail time.

**It 's a start--but even a start matters, because "equality for all" can no longer be a political phrase--mere words aren't enough.** A campaign that pledges solutions to the deep structural problems that have plagued our country for centuries can't slide by on lip service. A campaign that is truly about principles has to take those principles into the living rooms, memorial spaces, and houses of worship where those principles matter most.

**That 's why, after the NAN conference, I took a trip down to Alabama.** [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c8/522321320/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190411_JH_20190411-fr-JH-civilrights-all_all_i1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") The structure you see in this photo is the National Memorial for Peace and Justice, which opened just under a year ago in Montgomery, Alabama. In its own words, it is "the nation's first memorial dedicated to the legacy of enslaved black people, people terrorized by lynching, African Americans humiliated by racial segregation and Jim Crow, and people of color burdened with contemporary presumptions of guilt and police violence."

**Words cannot describe how breathtaking and heartbreaking the memorial is in person.** And I was fortunate to be there not just on my own, but with Reverend Patrick Demmer and the Reverend Dr. James Peters of the Denver Ministerial Alliance, who were invaluable partners to me as I led Colorado through crises ranging from police misconduct in Denver to our shooting tragedy in Aurora. Listening to and learning from these faith leaders evolved my thinking and made me a better public servant for our state. It was an honor to reflect on the memorial with them.

[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c9/522321320/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190411_JH_20190411-fr-JH-civilrights-all_all_i2&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") After visiting the memorial, it was onward to a warm welcome from the Alabama Dems. As the first presidential candidate to visit them, I spoke about the urgency of ending our crisis of division and getting progressive things done all over our country--including states like Alabama, whose rural health and incarceration crises have reached critical, untenable levels.

[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c6/522321320/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190411_JH_20190411-fr-JH-civilrights-all_all_i3&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") The final span of my listening tour was in South Carolina: visiting with the Beaufort Dems in Okatie, joining the joyous congregation of the Mount Moriah Baptist Church in Spartanburg, and--in a meeting both bittersweet and revelatory--spending time with survivors of the shooting at the Mother Emanuel Church in Charleston, where the twin evils of white supremacy and gun violence stole nine lives in 2015.

[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c7/522321320/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190411_JH_20190411-fr-JH-civilrights-all_all_i4&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Thanks to all the Southerners who shared their time, thoughts, and experiences so generously with me, I returned to Colorado with a full heart and new ideas for how to continue the work of repair. I also returned to news that, unfortunately, that work remains extensive: Between March 26 and April 4, three black churches in Louisiana were set ablaze. Those flames were a sobering reminder to me of our troubled past and our current responsibility. **We face, as MLK has said, a fierce urgency of now.** Our crisis of division--inflamed by assaults on minorities, the demonization of immigrants and the rising tides of anti-Semitism and Islamophobia, and a president who openly embraces white supremacists--will take a nationwide effort to heal. However, what I saw on this trip--the strength, resilience and optimism of the communities I met, as well as wise insights from faith and civil rights leaders who've spearheaded the work of repair for decades--gives me hope. Our situation is urgent. But it is also urgent that we not lose faith in each other and our ability to heal this crisis, together. [**_Our campaign is dedicated to that keeping that faith, and we 'll continue to pursue it in the coming months. If you'd like to support our principles of listening, learning, and rebuilding, please consider a small gesture of support today._**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c4/522321320/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190411_JH_20190411-fr-JH-civilrights-all_all_l1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Thank you,
John
[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c5/522321320/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190411_JH_20190411-fr-JH-civilrights-all_all_contribbutton&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

** **

Keep us company on the campaign trail by joining us on [_**Facebook**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c3/522321320/VEsP/ "https://www.facebook.com/JohnHickenlooper/"), [_**Twitter**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c0/522321320/VEsHBQ/ "https://twitter.com/hickenlooper"), [_**Instagram**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1c1/522321320/VEsHBA/ "https://www.instagram.com/johnhickenlooper/"), and [_**YouTube**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1fe/522321320/VEsHBw/ "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC82O0Byp_ny13O0LkO1UpUg/featured").
We love staying connected to you--but if at any point you'd like to opt out, you can do so right [here](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b1/691c9bd/6ca1a059/11a4b1ff/522321320/VEsHBg/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: I'm running for president in 2020. Here's why.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
I wanted to tell you why I decided to run for president in my own words. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637716b7/691c9bd/6ca1ae57/11a4b11a/1697861391/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend, It's official. I'm asking you to interview me to be your next president of the United States. That's the headline, but with you being one of my earliest supporters, I wanted to tell you why I decided to run, in my own words. Check out this video for the short and sweet version. [](https://secure.hickenlooper. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-mar?refcode=em_fr_20190304_JH_20190304-fr-JH-launch-all_all_i1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=") **Friend, we need a candidate who can beat Donald Trump and bring us together on the other side to make real progress. ** **We are facing a profound crisis, a crisis of division; we are more polarized than any time in generations.** Since Day 1 of his candidacy, Donald Trump has divided our country and threatened our democracy, attacked the middle class and alienated our allies. Under his administration, real people are being hurt. Real families are being separated. And Trump's reckless withdrawal from the Paris Climate Agreement has put our planet and our future under threat (an agreement we still adhere to in Colorado, by the way--as Governor, I made sure of it). **We hurtle from crisis to crisis as our democracy crumbles, but we never move forward --only farther apart.** We need a new leader--one who will restore our democratic norms, our international stature, and our national commitment to dignity and opportunity for all. But to actually get there, we need someone with a track record of getting people to put down their weapons, listen to each other, and work together in support of progress. **We need a leader who is, above all, about bringing people together to get things done --and I believe my experiences have prepared me to seek the progressive solutions our nation so urgently needs.** My own story is about facing challenges, large and small. When I lost my father at the age of 8, I started to live by one of my mom's guiding principles: **" You can't control what life throws at you, but you can control how you respond."** I grew up a skinny kid with a funny last name and coke bottle glasses, so I experienced my fair share of bullies. But I learned, with the help of humor and resilience, to never give up. After college, I became a geologist, mapping what lay beneath the earth's surface. I thought I had my life pretty figured out and all my boxes checked. But then, I was laid off--along with thousands of other geologists. **I lost not only my job, but also my profession. After a few months of unemployment, I started to see another person in the mirror. If you 've been laid off, you know how it goes: Everything you thought you knew and liked about yourself gets called into question.** But in time, with my mom's wisdom guiding me, I took back control. I got up. I got a library book on how to write a business plan, and I got to work. Eventually, with the help of friends, I turned a historic but abandoned building in Denver into the first brewpub in the Rocky Mountain West. Working together with other small businesses, we transformed twenty-six dilapidated blocks into a vibrant new neighborhood that never looked back. **The experience transformed me, too.** Throwing myself into neighborhood revitalization and community organizations led me to look around for bigger problems to help solve. And there were more than a few: In 2002, Denver itself faced a $70 million budget shortfall, a stagnating economy, and insufficient public transit. **I saw a chance to make a difference, so with the encouragement of my community, I ran for mayor --and won.** As mayor, together with leaders from surrounding communities, I tackled Denver's challenges. We got efficient public transit in place, we implemented criminal justice reform, and we even managed to balance our budget--without major service cuts or layoffs. Eventually, Denver became a national model for what a city could be. And I decided to keep going and see how I could serve Colorado as a whole. **I was humbled when the people of Colorado chose me as their Governor.** In my two terms, we continued to bring people together to pursue progressive goals and get things done: guiding our state through fires, floods, and recession, getting 95% of Coloradans insured, taking our economy from 40th in job creation to the number one economy in the nation, and passing some of the toughest environmental standards in the country. But there were bullies to stand up to then, too. When gun violence shattered Aurora, Colorado on July 20, 2012, the NRA tried to drown out the voices of people calling for action. I stood up to the NRA, and Colorado became the first western state to pass universal background checks and ban high-capacity magazines. **My second and final term as Colorado 's Governor ended in December, and since then, I've been listening****--****and people across the country have told me they are ready to move past divisive rhetoric and build a better future. That 's what I've done in Colorado, and that's what I want to do for our country. ** That's great, Hickenlooper, you say. So, what are the problems we need to tackle straight away? _Health care_ --**it 's a right, not a privilege.** _Tax fairness_ --**putting the middle class first,** not the wealthy few. _The future of the American worker_ --lifelong, affordable, versatile education so people can succeed in our changing economy. _Climate change_ --we need to rejoin the Paris Agreement and **transition to a renewable economy, stat.** _Civil rights and social justice_ --**equality, opportunity, security, and dignity for all Americans.** Donald Trump's rhetoric has torn us apart--we need to repair the seams. These are some of the most urgent problems we face--and together we can take them on while standing tall in the face of attacks and nonsense from the other side (including the bully in the White House). **Friend, it 's time to repair the damage done to our country. It's time to make our democracy work again. It's time to stand tall together and commit to a better path forward.** If you're ready to help me in realizing that vision for our country, show it today by joining our campaign. We're accepting no corporate PAC money--just people like you. _**[If you can, please chip in today. Your donation will represent more than a dollar amount--it will represent our first steps toward solutions to the challenges we face, and toward a better future for all.](https://secure.hickenlooper. That's the headline, but with you being one of my earliest supporters, I wanted to tell you why I decided to run, in my own words. Check out this video for the short and sweet version. "=" Friend, we need a candidate who can beat Donald Trump and bring us together on the other side to make real progress. We are facing a profound crisis, a crisis of division; we are more polarized than any time in generations. Under his administration, real people are being hurt. Real families are being separated. And Trump's reckless withdrawal from the Paris Climate Agreement has put our planet and our future under threat (an agreement we still adhere to in Colorado, by the way--as Governor, I made sure of it). We hurtle from crisis to crisis as our democracy crumbles, but we never move forward --only farther apart. We need a new leader--one who will restore our democratic norms, our international stature, and our national commitment to dignity and opportunity for all. But to actually get there, we need someone with a track record of getting people to put down their weapons, listen to each other, and work together in support of progress. We need a leader who is, above all, about bringing people together to get things done --and I believe my experiences have prepared me to seek the progressive solutions our nation so urgently needs. My own story is about facing challenges, large and small. When I lost my father at the age of 8, I started to live by one of my mom's guiding principles: " You can't control what life throws at you, but you can control how you respond." I grew up a skinny kid with a funny last name and coke bottle glasses, so I experienced my fair share of bullies. But I learned, with the help of humor and resilience, to never give up. After college, I became a geologist, mapping what lay beneath the earth's surface. I thought I had my life pretty figured out and all my boxes checked. But then, I was laid off--along with thousands of other geologists. I lost not only my job, but also my profession. After a few months of unemployment, I started to see another person in the mirror. name: Team Kaine
subject: SURVEY: What's on your mind?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Between the 2020 election already gearing up and the President's Twitter habit still going as strong as ever, sometimes day-to-day news can get a bit lost.
|
---
[ ](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f174e/3e0906/7be6f89b/3e30a6dc/774923377/VEsH/ "https://www.timkaine.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019&source=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019")

| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek:

Between the 2020 election already gearing up and the President's Twitter habit still going as strong as ever, sometimes day-to-day news can get a bit lost. When you turn on the TV at night, you may be more likely to hear about the latest outrageous comment than any actual updates about what's happening out in the world. But that doesn't mean there isn't plenty going on! Tim has been hard at work in the Senate -- [**and he'd love to hear from you about what issues matter the most to you right now**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f174e/3e0906/7be6f89b/3e30a6d3/774923377/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/news-and-issues-april-2019?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+and+hed+love+to+hear+from+you+about+what&utm_campaign=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019&source=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019").

Here's a short update on the issues Tim has had on his mind:
* There are some concerning developments in the US's relationship with Saudi Arabia: While both the House and Senate passed a resolution to end military support for Saudi Arabia's intervention in Yemen's civil war, President Trump is expected to veto it. The President still turns a blind eye to Saudi Arabia's human rights abuses, including the brutal murder of journalist and Virginia resident Jamal Khashoggi. And just recently, we learned about several approvals of secret transfers of nuclear technical information from American companies to Saudi Arabia. (You can read Tim's Twitter thread on this topic [here](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f174e/3e0906/7be6f89b/3e30a6d2/774923377/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/timkaine/status/1114516968390918144?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3+-+here&utm_campaign=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019&source=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019").)

* With Kirstjen Nielsen's departure from the Department of Homeland Security, there is a potential for our immigration policies to spiral even further out of control. Nielsen instituted cruel policies like family separation that led to thousands of children being ripped away from their parents. We don't yet know who will replace her.

* Republicans in the Senate recently voted to change the amount of time allotted to debate the confirmation of some judicial nominees, slashing the time from 30 hours to just two hours. This could allow fast-tracked confirmations for many of President Trump's judicial picks -- which would have implications on future rulings for decades to come.

* Tim introduced a new health care plan last week called Medicare-X, and he's been spending time since then talking with stakeholders and colleagues about how it would help ensure more options and lower costs. Medicare-X is a public plan that's built on the Medicare framework, but also ensures essential health benefits outlined in the Affordable Care Act, like maternity care and mental health services. It also works to lower prescription drug costs.

* In last year's budget, Congress authorized $700 million in student loan forgiveness for public servants like firefighters, nurses, and teachers. But we learned just last week that fewer than 300 people have been approved for relief by the Department of Education.

Those are just a few of the issues Tim's been thinking about this month. He's also been focused on introducing new legislation to the Senate, including a bill to support LGBTQ youth who experience abuse and a bill to prevent workplace harassment. But no matter how busy he gets in the Senate, Tim always wants to know what you think -- in fact, the busier things are in Washington, the more important it is to hear from you! Would you fill out this quick survey to let Tim know what issues are top of mind for you?
| ****[**TAKE THE SURVEY**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f174e/3e0906/7be6f89b/3e30a6d1/774923377/VEsC/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/news-and-issues-april-2019?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=4+-+TAKE+THE+SURVEY&utm_campaign=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019&source=20190412_1_SURV_TEAM_News-Issues-April-2019")****
---

Thank you so much. Your input is a critical part of Tim's work in the Senate and all around Virginia. We appreciate it!

-Team Kaine

The Tale of Two Buttigiegs
This Sunday, South Bend Mayor Pete Buttigieg will announce that he’s running for president. You may know him as the “small-city mayor with a middling record,” (as reported (https://www.wsj.com/articles/how-will-democrats-explain-south-bend-11554759834?mc_cid=f78379f657&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) in the Wall Street Journal), or potentially as the mayor with a failed tenure that includes rising poverty, eviction rates and crime.

But for years in Indiana, our former governor and now vice president, Mike Pence, called him a friend. They worked together on projects for the betterment of Northern Indiana and South Bend. But in recent weeks, Buttigieg has taken that friendly relationship and drug it through the mud, calling Vice President Pence “fanatical,” rejecting that he’s a “decent guy” and going as far to claim that he has a “quarrel” with God. And what has Vice President Pence said about Buttigieg in the past? Only that he holds him “in the highest personal regard,” and that he sees him as a “dedicated public servant and a patriot.”

It’s almost the tale of two Buttigiegs.

This week, Vice President Pence was asked again (https://www.cnn.com/2019/04/12/politics/mike-pence-pete-buttigieg-william-barr-julian-assange/index.html?mc_cid=f78379f657&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) what he thought of Buttigieg’s recent mudslinging. And his response was perfect: “I hope that Pete will offer more to the American people than attacks on my Christian faith or attacks on the President as he seeks the highest office in the land. He'd do well to reflect on the importance of respecting the freedom of religion of every American."

The question remains: If Buttigieg can’t effectively run a city of barely 100,000 Hoosiers here in the Heartland of America, how can he expect to lead a nation of more than 300 million?

The answer: He can’t.

GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS

1,100+ New Hoosiers Jobs Are Coming!

It was a BIG week for jobs and economic development in Indiana! Thanks to Governor Eric Holcomb’s leadership and Indiana’s pro-growth business environment, this week companies pledged to create 1,180 new Hoosier jobs in the coming years, with jobs announcements in Shelbyville, North Vernon, New Albany and Carmel. Check it out!
* Greenleaf Foods, SPC
+ Canada-based food company picked Shelbyville for North America's largest plant-based protein facility and up to 460 new Hoosier jobs
* Zotec
+ Medical revenue cycle management solutions provider will nearly double its headquarters footprint in Carmel, with plans to create up to 300 new jobs
* Decatur Plastics Products
+ Manufacturer of custom injection-molded plastics parts and components is expanding in North Vernon, creating up to 70 new jobs
* BCforward
+ Global technology consulting firm is expanding and opening a new digital training center in Carmel, creating up to 300 new jobs.
* HMS Global Maritime and American Queen Steamboat Company
+ Marine management and operations company is growing in New Albany and creating up to 50 new jobs.
